member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.CweInfo
member.summary: Maps a finding to its CWE (Common Weakness Enumeration) entry.

member.name: M:SharpGuard.Core.CweInfo.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Maps a finding to its CWE (Common Weakness Enumeration) entry.

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.CweInfo.Id
member.summary: CWE identifier, e.g. "89".

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.CweInfo.Name
member.summary: Short name, e.g. "SQL Injection".

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.CweInfo.Url
member.summary: Full URL to the CWE definition on mitre.org.

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Finding
member.summary: Represents a single security finding produced by a SAST rule analysis.

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.Finding.RuleId
member.summary: The rule identifier that produced this finding (e.g. "SG0001").

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.Finding.DiagnosticId
member.summary: The stable diagnostic ID (e.g. "SG0001").

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.Finding.Severity
member.summary: Severity assigned by the rule (may be overridden by configuration).

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.Finding.FilePath
member.summary: Absolute or relative path to the source file.

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.Finding.LineNumber
member.summary: 1-based line number where the issue starts.

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.Finding.ColumnNumber
member.summary: 1-based column number where the issue starts.

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.Finding.Message
member.summary: Human-readable description of the finding and remediation advice.

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.Finding.Cwe
member.summary: CWE classification for this finding.

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.Finding.CodeSnippet
member.summary: Optional snippet of the offending code (truncated if very long).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.ISecurityRule
member.summary: Defines the contract for a SAST security rule. Each rule detects one specific vulnerability pattern and is consumed by both the CLI and Roslyn Analyzer.

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.ISecurityRule.Id
member.summary: Unique rule identifier, e.g. "SG0001".

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.ISecurityRule.DiagnosticId
member.summary: Stable diagnostic ID used in Roslyn analyzers, e.g. "SG0001".

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.ISecurityRule.Title
member.summary: Short human-readable title, e.g. "SQL Injection".

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.ISecurityRule.Description
member.summary: Detailed description of the vulnerability being detected.

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.ISecurityRule.RemediationMessage
member.summary: Guidance on how to fix the vulnerability.

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.ISecurityRule.HelpLinkUri
member.summary: Link to documentation for this rule.

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.ISecurityRule.DefaultSeverity
member.summary: Default severity used when no user override is present.

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.ISecurityRule.Cwe
member.summary: CWE classification for this vulnerability.

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.ISecurityRule.Category
member.summary: Broad vulnerability category for grouping and filtering.

member.name: P:SharpGuard.Core.ISecurityRule.TriggerSyntaxKind
member.summary: The Roslyn that triggers analysis for this rule.

member.name: M:SharpGuard.Core.ISecurityRule.Analyze(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SyntaxNode,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Analyzes a single syntax node within its semantic context. Returns a if a vulnerability is detected, or null if the node is safe.

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.RuleFactory
member.summary: Central registry for all SAST rules. Both the CLI and Analyzer projects use this factory to obtain the rule set — avoiding duplication of rule registration.

member.name: M:SharpGuard.Core.RuleFactory.CreateAll
member.summary: Returns all registered SAST rules.

member.name: M:SharpGuard.Core.RuleFactory.GetRule(System.String)
member.summary: Looks up a rule by its .
member.returns: The rule, or null if not found.

member.name: M:SharpGuard.Core.RuleFactory.GetRuleByDiagnosticId(System.String)
member.summary: Looks up a rule by its .
member.returns: The rule, or null if not found.

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.CommandInjectionRule
member.summary: Detects command injection vulnerabilities where Process.Start is called with non-constant arguments that could allow arbitrary command execution (CWE-78).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.CrossSiteScriptingRule
member.summary: Detects potential Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities in ASP.NET applications, such as Html.Raw() or HttpResponse.Write with non-constant input (CWE-79).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.HardcodedSecretsRule
member.summary: Detects hardcoded secrets such as passwords, API keys, tokens, and connection strings in source code (CWE-798).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.InformationExposureRule
member.summary: Detects exception handling that exposes internal error details (exception message, stack trace) to end users (CWE-209).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.InsecureCookieRule
member.summary: Detects cookies created without Secure and/or HttpOnly flags, which could expose authentication tokens to interception or XSS (CWE-614).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.InsecureDeserializationRule
member.summary: Detects use of insecure deserializers such as BinaryFormatter, NetDataContractSerializer, and similar types (CWE-502).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.InsecureRandomRule
member.summary: Detects use of System.Random for security-sensitive purposes where a cryptographically secure random number generator is required (CWE-338).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.LdapInjectionRule
member.summary: Detects LDAP injection vulnerabilities where DirectorySearcher or DirectoryEntry accept non-constant LDAP filter strings (CWE-90).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.MissingAuthorizationRule
member.summary: Detects ASP.NET controller actions or minimal API endpoints that lack authorization attributes, potentially exposing functionality anonymously (CWE-306).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.OpenRedirectRule
member.summary: Detects open redirect vulnerabilities where HTTP redirects use non-constant URLs that could be controlled by attackers (CWE-601).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.PathTraversalRule
member.summary: Detects path traversal vulnerabilities where file system APIs are called with non-constant path arguments that may allow directory escape (CWE-22).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.SensitiveDataLoggingRule
member.summary: Detects logging of potentially sensitive data, such as variables containing passwords, tokens, or user data being written to log output (CWE-532).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.SqlInjectionRule
member.summary: Detects SQL injection vulnerabilities where SQL command text is built via string concatenation or interpolation with non-constant operands (CWE-89).

member.name: M:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.SqlInjectionRule.ResolveCommandText(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.InvocationExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.IMethodSymbol,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Attempts to resolve the expression that produces the command text. For SqlCommand, this could be the first constructor argument or a property setter.

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.SsrfRule
member.summary: Detects Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerabilities where HTTP clients are used with user-controlled URLs (CWE-918).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.TimeOfCheckTimeOfUseRule
member.summary: Detects TOCTOU (Time-of-Check Time-of-Use) race conditions where a file existence check is followed by a file operation without proper exception handling (CWE-367).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.TlsValidationRule
member.summary: Detects disabled TLS/SSL certificate validation, typically through ServerCertificateValidationCallback set to always return true (CWE-295).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.WeakCryptographyRule
member.summary: Detects use of weak cryptographic algorithms such as MD5, SHA1, DES, and ECB cipher mode (CWE-327).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.WeakRsaKeyRule
member.summary: Detects RSA keys created with fewer than 2048 bits, which are considered cryptographically weak (CWE-326).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.XPathInjectionRule
member.summary: Detects XPath injection vulnerabilities where XPath queries are built from non-constant expressions (CWE-643).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Rules.XxeRule
member.summary: Detects XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerabilities where XML parsers are configured with DTD processing enabled or external entity resolution allowed (CWE-611).

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.SecurityRuleBase
member.summary: Abstract base class for all SAST security rules. Provides a common method contract and helper utilities for creating instances.

member.name: M:SharpGuard.Core.SecurityRuleBase.Analyze(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SyntaxNode,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Analyzes a single syntax node within its semantic context. Returns a if a vulnerability is detected, or null if the node is safe.

member.name: M:SharpGuard.Core.SecurityRuleBase.CreateFinding(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SyntaxNode,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Helper to create a from the current analysis context. Automatically extracts file location from the node's syntax tree.
member.param: Optional code snippet; if omitted, truncated node text is used.

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.Severity
member.summary: Severity levels for security findings. Ordered from most to least critical.

member.name: F:SharpGuard.Core.Severity.Critical
member.summary: Immediate threat, must be fixed. Always error-level in analyzers.

member.name: F:SharpGuard.Core.Severity.High
member.summary: Serious vulnerability, should be treated as error in CI.

member.name: F:SharpGuard.Core.Severity.Medium
member.summary: Moderate risk, default warning.

member.name: F:SharpGuard.Core.Severity.Low
member.summary: Minor issue or best-practice violation, default info.

member.name: F:SharpGuard.Core.Severity.Info
member.summary: Informational only, default hidden in analyzers.

member.name: T:SharpGuard.Core.VulnerabilityCategory
member.summary: Broad categories for grouping security findings.

member.name: F:SharpGuard.Core.VulnerabilityCategory.Injection
member.summary: SQL, command, LDAP, XPath injection flaws.

member.name: F:SharpGuard.Core.VulnerabilityCategory.Cryptography
member.summary: Weak algorithms, insecure keys, predictable RNG.

member.name: F:SharpGuard.Core.VulnerabilityCategory.InformationDisclosure
member.summary: Secrets in code, verbose error messages, data leaks.

member.name: F:SharpGuard.Core.VulnerabilityCategory.Deserialization
member.summary: Unsafe deserialization of untrusted data.

member.name: F:SharpGuard.Core.VulnerabilityCategory.Configuration
member.summary: TLS/SSL misconfiguration, disabled certificate validation.

member.name: F:SharpGuard.Core.VulnerabilityCategory.Logging
member.summary: Sensitive data written to logs or traces.
